lens: integration QA: end-to-end flows, cross-crate composition, test-suite-accuracy-vs-shipping-product
headline: 20 of 473 Rust tests fail; 1 of 2 headline TS exit-criterion tests (scaffolder) fails; DSL wrapper double-stamps createdAt. Privileged-path exclusivity, transaction atomicity, nested-tx rejection, and most IVM propagation all hold. Most failures are legitimately Phase-2 (grant-backed caps, version-chain, IVM rebuild from event log) — but exit-criterion #3 (ON_DENIED on revoke) and exit-criterion #5-partner (scaffolder npm install) are headline Phase-1 gates that currently cannot be met.

integration_scenarios.ten_minute_dx_path: partial — engine + crud + IVM View 3 list ordering all work end-to-end for the Rust-side exit-criterion tests. TS-side `packages/engine` Vitest suite passes. BUT the scaffolder test (the headline `npx create-benten-app && npm install && npm test` path in QUICKSTART.md) fails at `npm install` because the generated package.json pins `@benten/engine: "*"` with no workspace-link / file: ref — the package isn't published. Scaffolder generates the project files correctly; the install step blows up. Also: the DSL wrapper double-stamps `createdAt` (once at registerSubgraph injector, once at call-time via `crud.stampCreatedAt()`) while the native side stamps it AGAIN via `created_at_seq` — three sources of truth for a single property.
integration_scenarios.subgraph_register_call_flow: pass — Compromise #8 genuinely closed at 25d2893. `Engine::dispatch_call` fetches handler CID, builds an op-specific Subgraph, walks it through `benten_eval::Evaluator` as the sole dispatch path, buffers host-side WRITEs into `pending_ops`, replays atomically inside a single transaction, and maps capability denial to ON_DENIED / system-zone to ON_ERROR uniformly. Evaluator is the dispatch path for CRUD, spec-registered SubgraphSpec, and the top-level `impl PrimitiveHost` call_handler — no fast-path bypass remains. 14 invariants check at registration via G6 invariants module (invariants 1/2/3/5/6/9/10/12 full; 8/11 stopgaps per plan scope).
integration_scenarios.privileged_paths_exclusivity: pass — 5/5 system_zone_api_exclusivity tests green. `Engine::create_node`, `Engine::transaction`, and subgraph WRITE all reject `system:*` labels with E_SYSTEM_ZONE_WRITE. `Engine::grant_capability`, `Engine::create_view`, `Engine::revoke_capability` are the sole writers of system-labeled Nodes, using `WriteContext::privileged_for_engine_api()` to bypass the guard. Integration-level test `handler_cannot_write_system_zone_via_normal_api` routes user forge-attempts through ON_ERROR with E_SYSTEM_ZONE_WRITE — correct.
integration_scenarios.error_routing_end_to_end: pass — drift-detect exits 0 with 44 TS codes / 34 Rust codes / 34 catalog codes aligned. `EngineError::code()` produces stable `'static str` mapping for all 30+ ErrorCode variants. `mapNativeError` in packages/engine routes napi errors to typed subclasses; errors.generated.ts has an error class per catalog code. Gap (not blocking): ChangeEvent's `actor_cid`/`handler_cid`/`capability_grant_cid` fields exist in the wire shape but the NoAuth path leaves them None, breaking `change_event_attribution_fields_populated` — Phase-2 wire-through deferred per R4b-rust-test-coverage F-equivalent.
integration_scenarios.cross_crate_contracts: partial — ChangeEvent preserves node payload through transaction replay (verified via `test_subscribe_all_change_events` + `ivm_ten_writes_reflected_in_list_in_order` which relies on stored Node's createdAt property read by View 3). View updates DO use `node.properties.get("createdAt")` first with `tx_id` fallback (content_listing.rs line 16-21 documents the Phase-1 contract). Cumulative iteration budget is wired through Evaluator — but compromise_1 (100-iter-batch TOCTOU bound) fails because `register_subgraph` rejects the 300-iter SubgraphSpec with InvRegistration (not InvIterateBudget) — either the test input is malformed for the current SubgraphSpec builder or the invariant is catching something unintended. Warrants investigation but flagged as minor integration issue.

pass1_disposition_map.r4-qa-1 TS infra missing: closed at pass-2 (packageJson files exist; vitest + workspace link functional; npm v11 assumption worked this run)
pass1_disposition_map.r4-qa-2 scaffolder workspace-link + rege: REGRESSED-but-narrow. Regex is fine. BUT `template/package.json:12` declares `"@benten/engine": "*"` (wildcard) not `file:` / `workspace:*`. Scaffolder generates file; the scaffolder TEST's `npm install` then fails because `@benten/engine` isn't published to npm. This is the headline 10-minute-DX exit-criterion gate (#5 per plan §1). See r4b-qa-1.
pass1_disposition_map.r4-qa-3 deterministic createdAt: closed — exit_2 asserts strict-monotonic across three writes + stamped-once re-read. Both properties hold in current implementation.
pass1_disposition_map.r4-qa-4 cross-process fidelity: closed — d2_cross_process_graph.rs spawns genuine child PID via `Command::new(env!(CARGO_BIN_EXE_...))`.
pass1_disposition_map.r4-qa-5 IVM lag tautology: closed at pass-2 — two-sided lag <= 1 + liveness check. Tests green.
pass1_disposition_map.r4-qa-6 fixture CID in compromise_4: closed — compromise_4 asserts canonical CID unchanged.
pass1_disposition_map.r4-qa-7 mermaid ?? fallback: closed — regressed at G8 mini-review fix-pass: `@mermaid-js/parser@0.6` proved unusable for `flowchart` subset, so dc396cf replaced the parser-based assertion with a structural regex. Mermaid.test.ts now green, but the rigor downgrade is worth flagging — see r4b-qa-4.
pass1_disposition_map.r4-qa-8 exit_3 builder semantics: REGRESSED. exit_3 builds with `.capability_policy_grant_backed()` (no-op per lib.rs:1607-1610) + `register_crud_with_grants` (aliased to register_crud per lib.rs:584-586). Neither actually enables grant-backed enforcement. NoAuthBackend permits both create AND the post-revocation create, so the `routed_through_edge("ON_DENIED")` assertion fails. See r4b-qa-2.
pass1_disposition_map.r4-qa-9 napi require() under Vitest ESM: closed — `createRequire(import.meta.url)` bridges CJS .node into ESM; bindings/napi Vitest green.
pass1_disposition_map.r4-qa-10 Engine::open vs builder drift: closed — Engine::open now delegates to EngineBuilder::new().open(path) at lib.rs:372-374.
pass1_disposition_map.r4-qa-12 E_TX_ABORTED edge pin: closed — ON_ERROR is the tx-abort routing edge; transaction_atomicity_end_to_end green.
pass1_disposition_map.p2-qa-1 workspace:* npm compat: partially-closed — vitest ran the suite successfully via the workspace wiring; 7/9 test files passed. scaffolder.test.ts still fails but for a different reason (wildcard dependency, not workspace protocol). See r4b-qa-1.
pass1_disposition_map.p2-qa-2 exit_3 composition semantics: NOT-CLOSED. The G4 brief disposition didn't actually land — capability_policy_grant_backed is still a no-op. exit_3 fails.
pass1_disposition_map.p2-qa-3 [[bin]] required-features: still-open. Compromise #7 documented the state; no regression test pins it (see r4b-rust-test-coverage r4b-rtc-3).
pass1_disposition_map.p2-qa-4 IVM barrier-synchronous visibili: closed-by-implementation. Engine's synchronous subscribe-then-commit wiring + the content_listing view actually observing every write before `Engine::call(...list)` returns means the lag <= 1 observed bound holds exactly at 0 for every write in the current test run.

findings.claim: Scaffolder test — the headline 10-minute-DX exit-criterion gate — fails at `npm install` because the generated package.json pins `@benten/engine: "*"` (wildcard) with no file:/workspace: alternative for the in-repo test path.
findings.detail: The scaffolder test at `tools/create-benten-app/test/scaffolder.test.ts:37-38` runs `npm install --silent --no-audit --no-fund` followed by `npm test` inside the generated `my-app/`. `npm install` fails (exit non-zero, aborting the test) because `@benten/engine` is not published to npm and the template has no workspace-protocol or file:-relative-path fallback for the in-repo test environment. The vitest CI lane reports this as a FAIL. QUICKSTART.md §1 positions `npx create-benten-app my-app && cd my-app && npm install && npm run dev` as the headline DX path — if the scaffolder's own integration test can't close that loop in CI, Phase 1 cannot credibly claim to ship it. Root cause was flagged at r4-qa-2 and p2-qa-1 and left unresolved; the fix landed for workspace wiring (bindings/packages/tools use `workspace:*`) but the TEMPLATE EMITTED TO USERS' GENERATED PROJECTS uses `"*"` wildcards instead of either `file:../packages/engine` (for test) or a strategy that self-configures at scaffolder time.
findings.suggested_fix: Option A (cleanest for Phase 1): scaffolder detects whether it's running inside the benten-engine monorepo (bin.mjs walks up looking for pnpm-workspace.yaml / root package.json `workspaces` + `"name": "benten-engine-workspace"`). If yes, emit package.json with `"@benten/engine": "workspace:*"`; if no, emit with a pinned version (e.g. `"^0.1.0"`) and mark it with a TODO referencing the unpublished-package follow-on. Option B (faster): change the test to exec `npm install --force` with a pre-generated `tarball` path, skipping registry resolution. Option A is the honest fix — the current test FAILING in CI is more informative than a hacked pass.

findings.claim: Exit-criterion #3 (capability denial routes to ON_DENIED) is FAILING. `Engine::builder().capability_policy_grant_backed()` is a documented Phase-1 no-op (`Self` passthrough — the default NoAuth policy stays in force). `Engine::register_crud_with_grants(label)` is identical to `register_crud(label)` (no grant-backed policy swap). So exit_3 builds an engine with NoAuthBackend in effect, `grant_capability` writes a system:CapabilityGrant Node that nothing reads, `revoke_capability` writes a system:CapabilityRevocation Node that nothing reads, and the post-revoke `call(...create)` succeeds because NoAuth permits it. The assertion `denied.routed_through_edge("ON_DENIED")` panics.
findings.detail: This cascades across 8 failing integration tests: exit_3_cap_denial_routes_on_denied, revoked_cap_routes_to_on_denied, compromise_1_toctou_window_bound_at_100_iter_batch, compromise_2_ecapdenied_read_leaks_existence, cap_toctou::capability_revoked_mid_eval_surfaces_at_batch_boundary, plus the 3 benten-caps tests (requires_enforcement {2x}, read_denial {2x}, ucan_stub_messages, toctou_iteration {2x}). The capability-gating primitive-level machinery exists (Engine's `impl PrimitiveHost::check_capability` calls `policy.check_write(&ctx)`; EngineTransaction collects `PendingOp`s for policy inspection at commit) but the GRANT-BACKED POLICY ITSELF doesn't exist — only NoAuth is wired. Per R4b-rust-test-coverage note, 11 of 20 Rust failures are the grant-backed-caps cluster, legitimately deferred per named Compromise #1/#4. Exit-criterion #3 is NOT a compromise — it's a Phase-1 exit gate per plan §1. Either the exit-criterion itself needs re-scoping to match the Phase-1 scope reality, or the grant-backed policy must land before Phase-1 closes.
findings.suggested_fix: Two valid paths, pick one: (A) Re-scope exit-criterion #3 from 'ON_DENIED on revoke' to 'typed-error-surface on unregistered-handler' (matching the TS-side template/smoke.test.ts which already made this substitution per the template file's own doc comment at line 56-62). Document that grant-backed capability denial is Phase 2. (B) Land a minimal GrantBackedPolicy in benten-caps that reads system:CapabilityGrant Nodes via the backend and denies writes whose required scope has no live grant, closing the test. Option A is honest about the current state; Option B is the spec-promised behavior. Both require a Phase-1 decision before R6.

findings.claim: The DSL wrapper double-stamps `createdAt`, and Rust triple-stamps it — three sources of truth for a single property. Currently only one of them sticks because `registerCrud` ignores the payload, but the code is fragile.
findings.detail: In `packages/engine/src/engine.ts`: (1) `registerSubgraph` with a crud handler builds an injector at line 246-256 that stamps `createdAt` on every WRITE's `args.properties` if absent. This injector is applied in `toNativePayload` at line 258 — but then (line 263) the code takes the `this.inner.registerCrud(crud.label)` path which IGNORES the payload entirely. The injected property is discarded. (2) `call` at line 382-389 stamps createdAt AGAIN on the input BEFORE dispatching. (3) Rust's `subgraph_for_crud` at lib.rs:922-930 stamps createdAt AGAIN on the WRITE's properties if the incoming property is absent. In the current test suite, call-time-stamp wins because the injected TS-side stamp (2) is present by the time Rust runs (3) sees it and skips. But (1) exists as dead code, (3) falls back to `created_at_seq` with different semantics (per-engine monotonic, not per-crud), and a future refactor that wires `registerSubgraph` payload through to Rust would produce a DOUBLE-INCREMENT collision. The wrapper is already drifting.
findings.suggested_fix: Kill the registration-time injector (engine.ts:243-256); the call-time stamp is sufficient. Document in a comment on `subgraph_for_crud` that the Rust-side stamp is a defense-in-depth fallback, not a primary path. Add a regression test that verifies `createdAt` is stamped exactly once per create call regardless of which layer supplied it.

findings.claim: Exit-criterion #4 topological-order assertion is vacuously passing. `HandlerPredecessors::predecessors_of` always returns `&[]`, so the loop at exit_4 lines 229-238 iterates zero predecessors per step and never checks ordering.
findings.detail: `HandlerPredecessors` is a Phase-1 stub (`_placeholder: ()`); its `predecessors_of(_node_cid)` returns `&[]`. The assertion `for pred in adj.predecessors_of(step.node_cid()) { assert!(seen.contains(pred), ...) }` iterates an empty slice every time, so the inner assertion never fires regardless of what order `trace.steps()` produces. Furthermore, `trace.steps()` synthesizes steps whose `node_cid` is all-zeros OR the created_cid — ALL steps share the same node_cid, so even if predecessors were populated, the `seen.contains(pred)` check would be satisfied after the first step regardless. Exit-criterion #4 as written is a 'trace has steps with duration_us > 0' test, not a topological-order test. The TS partner at packages/engine mermaid.test.ts was downgraded from parser-based to regex-based at G8 fix-pass commit dc396cf because @mermaid-js/parser@0.6 has no flowchart overload, so the TS mermaid validation is also structural-regex only — compounding rigor loss.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) populate `HandlerPredecessors` with the actual adjacency map read from the stored SubgraphSpec (non-trivial; Phase-2 as currently documented) and generate trace steps with per-primitive distinct CIDs (currently synthesized identically), OR (b) downgrade the test's doc comment to reflect what it actually asserts ('trace produces N steps with per-step timing > 0') and file Phase-2 exit-criterion-#4-proper. Option (a) is the spec-promised behavior; option (b) is honest about the current state. Do not leave the test as-is — a reader assumes topological ordering is validated when it is not.

findings.claim: `Engine::handler_to_mermaid(handler_id)` returns a CANNED 3-node `flowchart LR` regardless of the actual handler shape — not a render of the registered subgraph. Paired with trace-step synthesis (lib.rs:655-664) that hard-codes op→primitive-list mappings, the mermaid/trace surfaces do not reflect stored graph reality.
findings.detail: Per lib.rs:697-709, `handler_to_mermaid` only verifies the handler is registered, then returns a format!-constructed string with three hard-coded nodes `n0[READ]  n1[WRITE]  n2[RESPOND]` and two edges. Real CRUD handlers registered via `register_crud` are a READ+RESPOND or WRITE+RESPOND shape per the evaluator subgraphs synthesized in `subgraph_for_crud` (lib.rs:911-1036) — the mermaid output MISREPRESENTS what would execute. Trace step synthesis at lib.rs:655-664 does the same: hard-coded primitive-list-per-op mapping (`"create" => ["write", "respond"]`, `"list" => ["read", "respond"]`) rather than walking the actual registered subgraph. The downstream exit-criterion #5 regex check (`starts_with("flowchart ")` + contains `"-->"` + contains any of READ/WRITE/RESPOND) passes on any flowchart string with those substrings — completely divorced from correctness.
findings.suggested_fix: Render mermaid from the stored `SubgraphSpec` (when available) or the synthesized op-specific subgraph. Render trace steps by observing Evaluator's actual primitive-execution order via an instrumentation hook. Both are Phase-2-scoped per the code comments; the Phase-1 honest disposition is to name this compromise explicitly (Compromise #9 or similar) and add a regression test that pins the 'canned mermaid / synthesized trace' state so the day it becomes live, the test forces a re-review.

findings.claim: `post:list` fetches via `backend.get_by_label()` + sort by Node.createdAt — NOT via IVM View 3 state. IVM propagation tests measure the label-index scan, not the IVM view.
findings.detail: `Engine::read_view_with` (lib.rs:1286-1340) exists and consults `ivm.view_is_stale()`, but the CRUD `post:list` path goes through `outcome_from_terminal_with_cid` with a `list_hint` that at lib.rs:2028-2057 explicitly walks `engine.backend.get_by_label(hint)` and sorts by Node's `createdAt` property — it never touches the IVM subscriber's View 3 state. The `ivm_ten_writes_reflected_in_list_in_order` test passes because the backend's label index IS sorted by createdAt, not because View 3 is maintained. Verify: `read_view_strict("content_listing")` returns `Outcome{list: Some(Vec::new())}` on a healthy view (lib.rs:1316-1319) — the view returns EMPTY regardless of actual writes. So View 3 is wired up and receiving events (the subscriber fires, verified by `view_is_stale` plumbing) but its READ path returns empty; `post:list` reads from the backend index instead. The two are parallel, not integrated.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) route `post:list` through View 3's actual `read_page` API (currently only exposed at the benten-ivm crate level, not via Engine), OR (b) document that View 3 is a write-path sink for Phase-1 and reads go through the label index — closing the loop in Phase 2 when pagination becomes first-class. Option (a) is the spec-promised behavior per plan §2.7 row B6 and the ContentListingView doc comment.

findings.claim: `compromise_1_toctou_window_bound_at_100_iter_batch` fails with an InvRegistration error on the 300-iter SubgraphSpec BEFORE reaching the call_with_revocation_at assertion. The 300-iter ITERATE may exceed the invariants_3 fan-out bound or invariants_5 node count — but the error has no populated diagnostic fields, blocking diagnosis.
findings.detail: The RegistrationError returned at registration time has `kind: Registration` but `depth_actual: None, fanout_actual: None, nodes_actual: None, edges_actual: None, iterate_nest_depth_actual: None, violated_invariants: None` — the aggregate violation list is not populated. Either the aggregate-mode path isn't hit (non-aggregate paths surface only the first violation, and this test uses the default `register_subgraph`, not `register_subgraph_aggregate`) OR the invariant kind is one the diagnostic-field populator doesn't cover. Test fails at line 53's .unwrap() before exercising the TOCTOU batch-boundary logic under test.
findings.suggested_fix: Use `register_subgraph_aggregate` for diagnostic visibility OR reduce the iterate count under the invariant limit. Secondary: verify all RegistrationError construction paths populate the relevant diagnostic field (grep for `RegistrationError::new` and trace which invariants trigger it) — leaving them None makes this class of test failure undebuggable.

findings.location: crates/benten-engine/src/lib.rs:1776-1955 (impl PrimitiveHost for Engine — put_node / delete_node branches)
findings.claim: PrimitiveHost's put_node / delete_node / put_edge / delete_edge have a fallback branch (no active_call frame) that writes DIRECTLY to the backend, bypassing the transaction replay / capability hook. This is defensively scoped but creates an invariant hole.
findings.detail: Lines 1819-1828 (put_node), 1843-1847 (put_edge), 1857-1862 (delete_node), 1871-1877 (delete_edge) all contain a `drop(guard); self.backend.put_node(node)` branch when `active_call` is empty. Per the rustdoc, this is meant for 'Phase-1 code paths that call impl PrimitiveHost::put_node without a containing dispatch.' But any such call bypasses the capability hook entirely (no ops_collector), bypasses the change-broadcast (direct backend.put_node, not through Engine::create_node or Engine::transaction), and still emits a ChangeEvent because redb itself broadcasts on commit. It's a silent capability-check escape hatch. No test currently exercises it (impl PrimitiveHost is only called from Evaluator which is only called from dispatch_call, which always sets active_call), but 'defensive code that nothing calls' is a common source of future regressions.
findings.suggested_fix: Make the fallback branch return `EvalError::Backend("impl PrimitiveHost called outside a dispatch_call frame")`. If the defense isn't exercised it's safer to hard-fail than silently bypass capability. Add a test that explicitly calls `<Engine as PrimitiveHost>::put_node` outside a dispatch and asserts the error.

findings.claim: SECURITY-POSTURE.md documents ONLY Compromise #6 (BLAKE3 collision bound). CLAUDE.md and R4b-rust-test-coverage reference Compromises #1-#8 but #1/#2/#3/#4/#5/#7/#8 are absent from SECURITY-POSTURE.md. Compromise #8 was closed at 25d2893 per the commit message but there's no written-form record of the closure on the public security doc.
findings.detail: docs/SECURITY-POSTURE.md §'Named Compromises' has a single subsection for #6. The doc's own footer says 'Future compromises with security implications will be appended as sections here' — that append has not happened. Readers following the R1-Triage-Addendum reference to SECURITY-POSTURE.md for compromise detail will find only one of ~8.
findings.suggested_fix: Append sections for #1 (TOCTOU-batch-boundary cap refresh), #2 (ON_DENIED_READ leaks existence per option A), #4 (WASM runtime deferred), #7 ([[bin]] gating deferred), and mark #8 as 'Closed at commit 25d2893 — Engine::call now dispatches through Evaluator; CRUD fast-path removed'. Low-risk documentation work that restores the doc contract.

findings.claim: The napi `registerCrud` + `call` smoke test passes because it asserts only `typeof reportedCid === "string"` — not that the CID is valid, resolvable, or round-trips. A regression that returned a UUID or an empty string would still pass.
findings.detail: Line 100-101: `const reportedCid = outcome.createdCid ?? outcome.cid; expect(typeof reportedCid).toBe("string");`. No CID format check (`bafyr...` prefix), no `engine.getNode(reportedCid)` round-trip verifying the Node actually persisted, no label-index lookup. Test asserts the happy-path return shape, not the happy-path correctness. Compare with `ts_roundtrip_cid_matches_rust_fixture` (line 49-58) which asserts exact CID string + getNode round-trip + property equality — that's the bar.
findings.suggested_fix: Strengthen: after `engine.call(handlerId, "create", { title: "p1" })`, assert the CID is the canonical base32 shape (starts with `bafyr`), then `expect(engine.getNode(reportedCid).properties.title).toBe("p1")`. One extra line, vastly more confidence.

test_suite_accuracy_vs_shipping_product: exit_4 topological-order assertion is VACUOUS (HandlerPredecessors returns empty slice, trace steps share node_cid) — passes regardless of correctness. Flagged as r4b-qa-4.
handler_to_mermaid returns a CANNED flowchart unrelated to the registered subgraph, and the exit-5 regex assertion only checks substrings — passes regardless of correctness. Flagged as r4b-qa-5.
post:list goes through the backend label index, NOT View 3 — IVM propagation tests verify the index works, not that IVM is integrated. Flagged as r4b-qa-6.
napi ts_subgraph_register_and_call asserts only typeof string, not CID validity / round-trip. Flagged as r4b-qa-10.

r4-qa-5 tightening left the IVM lag test at `lag <= 1 + liveness` rather than `lag == 0`; in practice the current commit-synchronous wiring means lag is always 0, so the assertion is correct but weaker than it could be. Acceptable for Phase 1.

tests that DO validate-real-behavior (spot-checked): transaction_atomicity_end_to_end, privileged_paths (5/5 exclusivity tests), exit_2 createdAt monotonic + stamped-once, exit_6 canonical CID round-trip, cross_process_graph spawn-real-PID, nested_transaction_rejected with ?-propagation. These are well-constructed integration tests.

20 Rust test failures are 80% Phase-2 features asserted at R3 in red-phase (version-chain, grant-backed caps, IVM rebuild from event-log, UCAN stub routing) — legitimate red-phase tests waiting for Phase-2. Not a test-accuracy concern; a scope-completeness concern triaged by R4b-rust-test-coverage.
what_i_ignored: Rust unit-test quality (owned by rust-test-reviewer R4b pass)
Coverage completeness vs R2 landscape (owned by rust-test-coverage R4b pass; its 4 findings are disjoint from mine)
Rust lint / clippy / fmt gate (separate CI lanes)
benchmark performance targets (owned by future performance-engineer pass)
top_3_integration_concerns: Scaffolder test failure is a headline Phase-1 gate that currently cannot close. The DX promise at QUICKSTART.md §1 is not met — `npx create-benten-app && npm install` fails at step 1. Fix is one scaffolder-template tweak.

exit-criterion #3 (capability denial) is FAILING. Eight integration tests cluster on the same root cause: `capability_policy_grant_backed()` is a no-op stub. Phase-1 either needs a minimal GrantBackedPolicy OR must explicitly re-scope #3 to match what actually ships.

Three separate tests pass trivially (exit_4 topo, exit_5 mermaid, ts_subgraph_register_and_call CID shape). Each is cheap to strengthen; leaving them as-is encodes future regressions as passing. The handler_to_mermaid canned-string implementation is particularly regression-prone since any future evaluator change to produce REAL mermaid will not fire the test.
confidence: high — all 10 findings traceable to specific file:line references; integration scenarios verified by running cargo nextest (20 failures, inspected) + vitest (2 failures, inspected) + drift-detect (passing) + spot-reading dispatch_call / PrimitiveHost impl / subgraph_for_crud.
recommended_disposition: REVISE before R6 council. Two criticals (r4b-qa-1 scaffolder, r4b-qa-2 exit-3 stub) both have a clean Phase-1 decision fork: ship a minimal Phase-1 path (fix scaffolder template, re-scope exit #3) OR absorb the gap as a named compromise (document in SECURITY-POSTURE.md §Compromise #9/#10). Either fork is defensible; the current in-between state — headline exit-criterion tests failing without a written compromise — is not. Four majors (createdAt triple-stamp, topo vacuity, canned mermaid, post:list-bypasses-IVM) are all cheap fixes or honest downgrade-documentation. Four minors can fold into R5 touches during revision pass.
notes: The implementation is substantively solid — 453/473 Rust green, Compromise #8 genuinely closed, privileged paths truly exclusive, transaction atomicity holds, cross-process test spans real PIDs, IVM subscriber wires up and propagates events, DSL wrapper loads, createdAt injection works for exit_2. The weak spots cluster on the Phase-1/Phase-2 boundary: grant-backed caps (Phase-2 spec-wise but exit-criterion-wise Phase-1), canonical trace/mermaid (Phase-2 scoped in code comments but Phase-1 exit-wise), scaffolder integration (deliverable-wise Phase-1 but npm-publish-wise requires decision). Phase 1 has everything EXCEPT the closure of these boundary questions.
